content.text:
chores-09.md SG — natural boundary as you said. Tight retrospective focused on "what we built vs what we needed". Draft below; eyeball before I write the file.

---

# chores-09.md (proposed)

```markdown
# Chores-09.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..08.md;
09 starts here because chores-07 (0.42.0 cycle, 600+ lines) and
chores-08 (0.41.1 cycle, 1500+ lines) are both already large; the
init-clone-refactor rebase landing is a natural new-file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.

## init-clone-refactor rebase landing (0.42.0-4.7)

Rebased main (0.42.0-0..-4.6) onto init-clone-refactor at its
0.41.1 close-out tip (`slvprlpw`). Chids preserved; ochid
trailers in the rebased commits stayed structurally valid (the
.claude pairings they point at had been WC-only during the icr
divergence; -4.7 itself re-establishes the .claude side).

### What needed real work

- **ttzwvpoq (0.42.0-1):** full `Scope` enum lifted wholesale
  from pre-rebase main; `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `is_bot_only`
  and `Single` / `parse_scope` since icr's bundle design keeps
  them dormant.
- **mvusyowm (0.42.0-2):** icr's bundle-flatten `InitArgs` +
  `ScopeOption`/`ScopeKind` already supersedes mvusyowm's
  parser-switch + `Option<Scope>` design; took icr wholesale
  across all 5 init.rs conflict regions. The cycle's narrative
  intent (custom `--scope` parser accepting paths) becomes
  dormant on the icr base; the `Scope::Single` variant survives
  for future use.
- **kyurxpnu (0.42.0-4.5):** CLAUDE.md substep references merged
  (lulqxovr's icr-side decomposition pointer + kyurxpnu's
  external `substep-protocol.md` / `jj-revsets.md` links) into
  a single continuation paragraph under `### Versioning`.
- **Reference renumbering** — cycle's `[72]`/`[73]`/`[74]`
  collided with icr's chores-08 anchors: `[72]→[76]`,
  `[73]→[77]`, `[74]→[78]`, plus self-refs in chores-07's
  -4.5 and -4.6 sections.

### Precautions taken (most unnecessary in retrospect)

- `main-2` local duplicate bookmark — never read.
- `gca-icr-main` common-ancestor marker — never referenced.
- `rslv-commit` cursor bookmark — handy as a navigation
  aid during cascade, not load-bearing (`@-` works).
- `../vc-x1-main` + `../vc-x1-icr` reference clones — useful
  for content lookup at specific chids; replaceable with
  `jj log -r <chid> --patch` against the local repo.
- Filesystem snapshots `vc-x1-20260505-1`, `vc-x1-20260506-1`
  — never restored from.
- `~/vc-x1-rebase-status.md` scratch file — useful for
  resuming across sessions, but ~200 lines was over-detailed;
  a 30-line cursor + decisions log would have sufficed.

### What was load-bearing

- `jj op restore` for local rewinds.
- origin's canonical pre-rebase main remaining intact until
  the explicit force-push at the end (the only fallback that
  mattered).
- jj's chid-preservation across rebase: ochid trailers stay
  valid pointers without manual fixup.
- Per-commit cargo cycle (fmt/clippy/test/install --locked)
  to verify each squash kept the chain buildable.

### Distilled recipe for next time

1. `jj rebase` of the divergent chain onto the new base.
2. Per conflicted commit: `jj new <commit>`, edit conflicts,
   cargo cycle, `jj squash`.
3. After cascade clears: cargo cycle at tip, `jj git push
   --bookmark main`.
4. `.claude` side: `vc-x1 push` to author the paired commit
   once main converges with origin.

Skip the duplicate bookmarks, reference clones, and
filesystem snapshots unless a specific reason emerges. The
op-log + origin are the safety net.
```

---

~70 lines. Tone matches your "experienced now, didn't need all that" framing.

Tweaks possible:
- More/less detail on specific resolutions (e.g., note the kyurxpnu CLAUDE.md merge picked option (A) of three).
- Include a bookmarks-cleanup section listing what to delete (`main-2`, `gca-icr-main`, `rslv-commit`, `.claude/init-clone-refactor`).
- Drop a section if you find it redundant.

OK to write `notes/chores-09.md` with this content?

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

### 0.42.0-4.6 WIP — init-clone-refactor recovery (2026-05-02 EOD)

Committed mid-flow. Pick up tomorrow.

Status: notes / `Cargo.toml` edits in place; recovery
itself is done (branch `init-clone-refactor` clean at
`a8c1eefe`, 331/331 tests pass). What's *not* yet in
-4.6:

- **Top-of-Todo entry for "Finish `init-clone-refactor`
  cycle"** — drafted in conversation, not yet added.
  Goal: DRY init+clone (consolidate `-6.x` substep
  ladder per `notes/substep-protocol.md` close-out
  recipe → finish any remaining planned work → ship
  0.41.1) before `--scope` rolls out across more
  commands. Position: top of `## Todo`, above the
  merge-direction entry.

After that's added, -4.6 is shippable; cycle returns
to planned 0.42.0-5 (finalize --scope).

### 0.42.0 cycle ladder

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.

213	   `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
214	   `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
215	   `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
216	   resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
217	   `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
218	   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
219	   `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
220	   Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
221	   project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
222	   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
223	   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
224	   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
225	   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
226	   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
227	   it adds value over the resolution chain.

that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]

1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]
1. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
   `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
   `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
   `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
   resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
   `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
   `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
   Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
   project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
   it adds value over the resolution chain.

## Done

and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

content.subject: Resolve mvusyowm conflicts (3 files)
content.description: Edit conflict markers in WC for notes/todo.md, src/scope.rs, src/init.rs. notes/todo.md done.

content.subject: Plan src/init.rs region-by-region resolution
content.description: 5 conflict regions, ~730 lines. Read each region in WC + ../vc-x1-main (mvusyowm pre-rebase) + ../vc-x1-icr (new base) and plan before editing.

content.subject: Cargo cycle + squash mvusyowm
content.description: fmt + clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings + test + install --locked, then jj squash -R . to fold WC into mvusyowm.

content.subject: Advance rslv-commit to plyvnpxt
content.description: Move rslv-commit cursor to next conflicted commit (plyvnpxt = 0.42.0-3 sync --scope retrofit). User advances ../vc-x1-main alongside.

content.subject: Resolve plyvnpxt → lulqxovr → kyurxpnu → uxymmmyn
content.description: 4 remaining conflicted commits in the cascade. Each gets: jj new rslv-commit, edit conflicts, cargo cycle, squash, advance.

content.subject: Final cargo cycle at main tip + force-push
content.description: Sanity cargo cycle at uxymmmyn, then force-push main (deferred hard-to-reverse step). User-gated.

- **todo.md `## In Progress`**: Removed the stale `### 0.42.0-4.6 WIP` block; collapsed "0.42.0 cycle ladder" subsection back into a flat ladder. Added sub-step lines under -4: `-4.5 (done) [77]`, `-4.6 (done) [78]`, `-4.7 (current) [79]`. Dropped the `(current)` marker on -4 itself (active sub-step is -4.7).

- **todo.md References**: Added `[79]: /notes/chores-09.md#init-clone-refactor-rebase-landing-0420-47`. The other refs ([71]-[75]) stay since they're still used by `## Todo` entries.
- **done.md**: Appended the 13 migrated 0.40.0-1 / 0.41.0 / 0.41.1 entries before `# References`. Added `[71]`-`[75]` ref targets at the end.

Note: I dropped the `(current)` marker on `-4 push --scope` since the active work is happening at the sub-step level (`-4.7`). Single-current convention. If you'd rather keep `-4 (current)` as the parent-step marker, easy revert.

Cargo cycle clean at 0.42.0-4.7.

## Step 4 (final) — `vc-x1 push main`

Proposed command:

```
vc-x1 push main --yes \
  --title "chore: init-clone-refactor rebase landing (0.42.0-4.7)" \
  --body "Re-establishes ochid pairing on .claude after the 0.42.0 cycle ran without .claude pairings during the init-clone-refactor divergence. Bundles the rebase-landing retrospective + Done cleanup.

- notes/chores-09.md: new file with rebase-landing retrospective (precautions taken vs what was load-bearing; distilled recipe).
- notes/todo.md: ladder shows -4.5/-4.6/-4.7 sub-steps under -4; ## Done trimmed to 0.42.0 entries; [79] ref added.
- notes/done.md: 13 pre-0.42.0 entries migrated; [71]-[75] refs added.
- Cargo.toml: 0.42.0-4.6 → 0.42.0-4.7."
```

`--yes` skips the review gate (we've inspected via dry-run + reviewed each edit). Title/body inline skips the editor gate. Drop `--yes` if you want either gate interactive.

Final state preview after this lands:
- App: new commit on top of uxymmmyn with the title above + ochid pointing at .claude's new commit
- `.claude`: paired commit with same title/body + ochid pointing at app's new commit, capturing 1491+ lines of session activity
- Both bookmarks pushed to origin
- Finalize-claude detached, captures any trailing session writes

OK to run?

Local cleanup complete. Status:
- App: 3 local-only bookmarks deleted (`gca-icr-main`, `main-2`, `rslv-commit`); `init-clone-refactor` marked deleted (origin still has it).
- `.claude`: `init-clone-refactor` marked deleted (origin still has it).
- `~/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1-rebase-status.md` removed.

## Push deletion to origin

To complete removal of `init-clone-refactor` from both remotes:

```
jj git push --deleted -R .
jj git push --deleted -R .claude
```

This is the destructive step — origin's `init-clone-refactor` branch is gone after this. Recovery would require recreating from local history. The branch's tip (slvprlpw on app, xnwpntyy on .claude) is still reachable as ancestors of `main` on both sides, so chids/commits aren't lost.

OK to push deletions to both origins?
